back_translation: Then Solomon began to build the house of the LORD in Jerusalem, on Mount Moriah, where the LORD had appeared to David his father, at the place that David had prepared, on the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.
He began the construction on the second day of the second month, in the fourth year of his reign.
These are the foundations that Solomon laid for building the house of God: the length was sixty cubits and the width twenty cubits, according to the old standard of measure.
The portico at the front, across the width of the house, was twenty cubits long and one hundred twenty cubits high; and he overlaid the inside with pure gold.
As for the main hall, he paneled it with cypress wood, then overlaid it with fine gold, and decorated it with designs of palm trees and chains.
He adorned the house with precious stones for beauty; and the gold was gold from Parvaim.
He overlaid the house — the beams, the thresholds, the walls, and the doors — with gold, and he carved figures of cherubim on the walls.
He built the Most Holy Place; its length, across the width of the house, was twenty cubits, and its width twenty cubits; and he overlaid it with fine gold weighing six hundred talents.
The gold nails weighed fifty shekels; and he also overlaid the upper rooms with gold.
In the Most Holy Place he made two cherubim by the work of a craftsman's carving, and overlaid them with gold.

The wings of the two cherubim together were twenty cubits long: one wing of the first cherub was five cubits and touched the wall of the house, and its other wing was five cubits and touched the wing of the other cherub.
One wing of the second cherub was five cubits and touched the wall of the house, and its other wing was five cubits and touched the wing of the other cherub.
The wings of these cherubim spread out twenty cubits in all; the cherubim stood upright on their feet, facing the main hall.
He made the veil of blue, purple, and crimson yarn and fine linen, and embroidered figures of cherubim on it.
He made two pillars in front of the house, thirty-five cubits high, and the capital on the top of each pillar was five cubits.
He made chains in the inner room and put them on the tops of the pillars; and he made one hundred pomegranates and fastened them to the chains.
He set up the two pillars in front of the temple, one on the right (the south) and one on the left (the north); the pillar on the right he named Jachin, and the pillar on the left he named Boaz.
